records.ety: [F. dmanteler, OF. desmanteler; pref: des- (L. dis-) + manteler to cover with a cloak, defend, fr. mantel, F. manteau, cloak. See Mantle.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dismantled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Dismantling (?).]

defs: To strip or deprive of dress; to divest.
To strip of furniture and equipments, guns, etc.; to unrig; to strip of walls or outworks; to break down; as, to dismantle a fort, a town, or a ship.
quotes: A dismantled house, without windows or shutters to keep out the rain.
